_comment: Reference config from the OpenClaw Optimization Guide. Updated for the 2026.6.4 stable / 2026.6.11-beta.1 era. Copy to ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json (or your project root) and edit. Never commit real credentials — use env var references like ${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}. Treat this as a starting point; provider catalogs/manifests should own durable model rows where possible.

model._comment: `opus` aliases resolve to Claude Opus 4.7 on current Anthropic/Bedrock routes. Codex app-server routes should use canonical openai/gpt-* refs from your local provider catalog; do not pin durable openai-codex/* or codex-cli/* model refs. Do not assume Claude Pro/Max subscription usage covers OpenClaw after the April 4 cutoff; set budget caps on paid routes.

defaults._budget_comment: 2026.6.4+: per-agent budget caps. onExceed: warn | degrade | stop. `degrade` reroutes to the cheapest fallback lane — only useful if fallbacks are configured. Override per agent in agents.list[].budget.
defaults._imageQuality_comment: 2026.5.24-beta.1: token-efficient | balanced | high-detail. Keep balanced unless a worker truly needs high-detail OCR/vision.

compaction._comment: Keep compaction on a cheap, fast, non-reasoning model. The transcript byte guard prevents runaway compaction on huge sessions.

memorySearch._comment: Local Ollama is the right default. Switch to `copilot` only if your org already pays for Copilot Business/Enterprise and accepts cloud-latency memory search.

contextPruning._comment: Stops SOUL+AGENTS+MEMORY re-injection every turn. Single biggest latency win on frontier models.

list._comment: 2026.5.20+: prefer per-agent lean mode for small local workers instead of forcing the orchestrator lean globally.

recall._comment: 2026.6.4+: bound concurrent memory sub-agents. Raise on a fast local embedding tier; lower on shared hardware so recall doesn't starve foreground turns.

dreaming._schedule_comment: 2026.6.4+: explicit off-hours schedule instead of idle-only. Verify promotions with `memory promote --dry-run` before trusting them.

storage._comment: Current default. Phase blocks land in memory/dreaming/{phase}/YYYY-MM-DD.md. Flip to `inline` if you want them in the daily memory file.
Switch to `cloud` with S3-compatible creds if you want durable memory indexes on object storage. Keep embeddings local unless you accept 2-5s memory-search latency.

config._comment: Enable per surface. Deny public/group channels until you have a retention policy.

policy._comment: 2026.5.20+: run openclaw policy check before inviting shared-channel users.

meeting-notes._comment: 2026.5.22+ source-only external plugin; 2026.6.4 adds Google Meet live capture plus retentionDays/redaction. Define retention before enabling any live source.

messages._comment: Current May line: steer is the default active-run queue mode. Use collect/followup/interrupt only when intentional. ambientTurns=room_event should collect context quietly; visible replies still go through message tools.

tools._comment: Defense-in-depth for channel users. Sender identities must come from the channel adapter, not message text. Replace the discord id with your own admin/channel sender key.

anthropic._comment: 2026.6.4+: health-checked failover. A lane that crosses failureThreshold is demoted for demoteForMs, then re-promoted. Only helps if fallback lanes exist (see agents.defaults.model.fallbacks).

openrouter._comment: 2026.5.20+: provider-level OpenRouter routing policy; model/agent params can override.

ollama-local._comment: 2026.5+: provider-level localService can start local model daemons on demand. Remove this block if Ollama is managed elsewhere.

repo-tools._comment: 2026.5.16+: scope user MCP projection into native Codex to named OpenClaw agents. Do not expose every MCP server to every Codex run.

codex._comment: 2026.5.16+: make native Codex tool approvals explicit (auto | prompt | approve).

audit._comment: 2026.5.18+: only suppress reviewed audit findings with owner/reason/expiry in your real config.

rotation._comment: 2026.6.4+: openclaw doctor warns on credentials older than this so stale long-lived keys surface in routine health checks.

skills._comment: Auto-update ON is the sleeper-update attack. Pin specific refs: `openclaw skills install author/skill --ref v1.2.0`. See Part 23.

approvals._comment: Semantic categories replaced name-based allowlists in 2026.3.31-beta.1. See Part 24 for per-agent overrides.

auto-capture._comment: Extracts claim-named knowledge notes into vault/00_inbox/. See Part 11 and Part 29 for current hook registration.
